author: Ameen Hassan
summary: Major session focused on restoring the discovery sentinel pipeline to a pre-Bedrock stable state after instability was detected. Context included reviewing IDeaS org GitHub documentation and Entra ID screenshots. Key issue: the pipeline had no stable baseline because Bedrock step E was producing inconsistent results. Session involved reorganizing the repo so that both the Bedrock approach and a no-Bedrock fallback could coexist, removing any references to Claude/Anthropic from the codebase, and working out how to merge cleanly into the ideasorg enterprise repo (which requires a branch-first workflow due to Datadog review). Session ran a 360Chicago session 1 test and observed 23% coverage — lower than expected — prompting investigation of pipeline stability before proceeding with further runs.
problems: Pipeline unstable after Bedrock experiments — no clean baseline
No references to Claude or Anthropic should appear in the repo (org policy)
ideasorg enterprise repo requires merging via branches due to Datadog review process
360Chicago session 1 returned only 23% coverage — unexpected and concerning
Repo had conflicting files that could confuse both Bedrock and non-Bedrock approaches
approaches: Reviewed IDeaS GitHub org documentation to understand merge and workflow constraints
Reviewed Entra ID configuration screenshots for OAuth context
Reorganized repo so both Bedrock and no-Bedrock approaches could coexist without conflicts
Removed all Claude/Anthropic references from codebase
Created a branch, reviewed it against ideasorg/main, and planned the merge
Ran 360Chicago session 1 through the pipeline to test stability
results: Pipeline restored toward stable pre-Bedrock baseline
360Chicago session 1 completed with 23% coverage — diagnosed as potential pipeline instability issue

decisions.decision: Must fix underlying pipeline instability before running any future sessions — no point running if output is unreliable
Repo must support both Bedrock and non-Bedrock approaches without contradicting files

open_questions: Why did 360Chicago session 1 return only 23% coverage — is this a genuine gap or a pipeline bug?
Are the merges happening in ideasorg or the personal fork?
